agent-frameworks-tooling-and-sandboxed-l.title: Agent Frameworks, Tooling, and Sandboxed LLM Workflows
agent-frameworks-tooling-and-sandboxed-l.when_to_read: LLM agent frameworks, browser automation agents, tool calling, graph/state runtimes, hosted or sandbox execution, MCP, CLIs, and production agent operations.
agent-frameworks-tooling-and-sandboxed-l.how_to_choose: Choose the repo skill whose framework, runtime, tool protocol, or agent execution surface most directly matches the request; use package names and API terms as the strongest signal.
agent-frameworks-tooling-and-sandboxed-l.split_guidance: Keep agent SDKs, MCP-enabled apps, browser agents, and graph/state agent runtimes together unless a future cluster has several repos with a distinct operational surface.

ai-gateway-and-llm-provider-integration.title: AI Gateway and LLM Provider Integration
ai-gateway-and-llm-provider-integration.when_to_read: OpenAI-compatible provider routing, gateway/proxy servers, model endpoint mapping, virtual keys, spend controls, pass-through APIs, and provider SDK troubleshooting.
ai-gateway-and-llm-provider-integration.how_to_choose: Use this scenario for provider routing and gateway/proxy concerns; use agent-framework scenarios when the user is primarily building an agent workflow.

ai-memory-sdks-and-app-integration.title: AI Memory SDKs and App Integration
ai-memory-sdks-and-app-integration.when_to_read: Persistent memory SDKs, memory search/update/delete flows, self-hosted memory services, memory CLIs, and agent memory integrations.
ai-memory-sdks-and-app-integration.how_to_choose: Choose the memory skill for persistent user or application memory, and then use its sub-skill routes for SDK calls, CLI operations, or self-hosted deployment.

ai-pipelines-workflows-and-rag.title: AI Pipelines, Workflows, RAG, and Agents
ai-pipelines-workflows-and-rag.when_to_read: Pipeline and workflow frameworks that combine embeddings, LLMs, agents, task chains, services, and RAG orchestration.
ai-pipelines-workflows-and-rag.how_to_choose: Use this scenario when the package is primarily an end-to-end AI workflow system rather than only a retriever, vector database, or model-serving backend.

bulk-rna-seq-differential-expression-ana.title: Bulk RNA-Seq Differential Expression Analysis
bulk-rna-seq-differential-expression-ana.when_to_read: Bulk RNA-seq raw-count differential expression analysis, DESeq2-like Python workflows, count/metadata validation, contrasts, Wald tests, LFC shrinkage, VST, and PyDESeq2 troubleshooting.
bulk-rna-seq-differential-expression-ana.how_to_choose: Use this scenario for package-specific bulk RNA-seq DEA workflows. Do not route general Scanpy/single-cell tasks here unless the user explicitly asks for PyDESeq2 or bulk count differential expression.
bulk-rna-seq-differential-expression-ana.split_guidance: The existing single-cell omics scenario centers on AnnData/Scanpy/scvi single-cell workflows, while PyDESeq2 covers bulk RNA-seq DESeq2-like raw-count differential expression. The scientific-python data scenario is too broad and would hide domain-specific contrast, count-schema, and DEA result-routing needs. Other bulk RNA-seq DEA, DESeq2-wrapper, edgeR/limma-like Python workflow skills can share this scenario.

computer-vision-modeling-and-augmentatio.title: Computer Vision Modeling and Augmentation
computer-vision-modeling-and-augmentatio.when_to_read: Image augmentation, PIL/Pillow operations, vision datasets/transforms, classification and detection models, segmentation, YOLO/SAM/OpenMMLab workflows, and model export.
computer-vision-modeling-and-augmentatio.how_to_choose: Choose by the named package and task shape: augmentation for Albumentations, image primitives for Pillow/TorchVision/TIMM, detection or segmentation for Detectron2/MMDetection/MMSeg/SAM/GroundingDINO, and export only when tied to a vision model workflow.

data-model-customization.title: Data, Model, Registry, and Template Customization
data-model-customization.when_to_read: Dataset schemas, message rows, column mappings, model/template registries, custom datasets/models/templates, and package-specific data customization workflows.
data-model-customization.how_to_choose: Use this scenario when the request is primarily about adapting data or model registries for a specific ML framework; choose dataset-processing scenarios for generic dataset loading or evaluation plumbing.

data-orchestration-and-parallel-computin.title: Data Orchestration and Parallel Computing
data-orchestration-and-parallel-computin.when_to_read: Workflow engines, DAGs, assets, Snakefiles, lazy/parallel task graphs, distributed arrays/dataframes, feature stores, cloud compute orchestration, and production data pipelines.
data-orchestration-and-parallel-computin.how_to_choose: Choose by execution substrate: Airflow/Dagster/Prefect/Snakemake for workflow orchestration, Dask for parallel Python data compute, Feast for feature stores, SkyPilot for cloud compute jobs, and PyG/DGL only when graph-data workflows are central.

dataset-processing-evaluation-and-hub-wo.title: Dataset Processing, Evaluation, and Hub Workflows
dataset-processing-evaluation-and-hub-wo.when_to_read: Dataset loading, schemas, streaming, format conversion, metric/evaluator modules, benchmark task selection, result caches, data validation, and Hub or CLI workflows.
dataset-processing-evaluation-and-hub-wo.how_to_choose: Use dataset and evaluation packages here when the request is about data or metrics plumbing; route model benchmark harnesses to language-model-evaluation-workflows when LLM evaluation configuration is primary.

deep-learning-training-engines.title: Deep Learning Training Engines
deep-learning-training-engines.when_to_read: Tasks about engine-level utilities, runners, hooks, loops, config systems, logging, visualization, and debugging for deep learning training frameworks.
deep-learning-training-engines.how_to_choose: Use this scenario for framework engine internals such as MMEngine; choose model-family scenarios when the request is about a concrete model workflow.

distributed-pytorch-training-and-large-m.title: Distributed PyTorch Training and Large Model Workflows
distributed-pytorch-training-and-large-m.when_to_read: Distributed training loops, launchers, DeepSpeed/FSDP/TPU backends, PyTorch Lightning/Fabric, scalable GNN training, checkpoints, and memory planning.
distributed-pytorch-training-and-large-m.how_to_choose: Choose Accelerate for generic distributed loop migration and launch, DeepSpeed for ZeRO/DeepSpeed configs, Lightning for Lightning/Fabric, PyG for graph neural training, OpenRLHF/verl for RLHF-scale training stacks, and Optuna for hyperparameter optimization.

document-processing-unstructured-data-an.title: Document Processing, Unstructured Data, and RAG Preparation
document-processing-unstructured-data-an.when_to_read: Document conversion, partitioning, OCR/table pipelines, Markdown conversion, document elements, chunking, extraction, cleaning, staging, remote document services, and RAG-ready document preparation.
document-processing-unstructured-data-an.how_to_choose: Choose by document surface: MarkItDown for conversion to Markdown and MCP conversion, Marker for PDF/OCR extraction, Docling or Unstructured for richer document element pipelines.

embedding-retrieval-rag-workflows.title: Embedding Retrieval and RAG Workflows
embedding-retrieval-rag-workflows.when_to_read: Embedding models, semantic or sparse search, reranking, vector or graph RAG, retrieval training, corpus indexing, RAG pipelines, vector database clients, and retrieval evaluation.
embedding-retrieval-rag-workflows.how_to_choose: Choose the repo skill whose package owns the retrieval layer named by the request: vector client, retriever model, sparse index, graph RAG system, RAG framework, or benchmark harness.

embodied-ai-simulation.title: Embodied AI Simulation and Training
embodied-ai-simulation.when_to_read: Embodied AI simulation, navigation/rearrangement tasks, habitat-style configs, datasets, training, HITL apps, and simulator extension registries.
embodied-ai-simulation.how_to_choose: Use this scenario for embodied AI simulation and training stacks rather than generic reinforcement learning algorithms or vision models.

genomics-hts-file-workflows.title: Genomics HTS File Workflows
genomics-hts-file-workflows.when_to_read: SAM/BAM/CRAM alignment files, VCF/BCF variant files, tabix-indexed genomic interval tables, FASTA/FASTQ sequence access, htslib/samtools/bcftools Python wrappers, and high-throughput sequencing file troubleshooting.
genomics-hts-file-workflows.how_to_choose: Choose the repo skill whose package or API surface owns the requested genomics file workflow: pysam for Python htslib/samtools/bcftools bindings, alignment/variant/tabix/FASTA/FASTQ file APIs, and command-wrapper troubleshooting.
genomics-hts-file-workflows.split_guidance: Existing scientific and molecular scenarios focus on generic scientific arrays, molecular simulation, chemistry, protein, materials, or omics analysis workflows; they do not capture high-throughput sequencing file formats, htslib index mechanics, samtools/bcftools wrappers, or genomic interval coordinate troubleshooting. Future skills for HTSlib bindings, BAM/CRAM/VCF utilities, genomic interval indexing, sequence-file APIs, or command wrappers around samtools, bcftools, tabix, bgzip, and faidx can share this scenario.

graph-learning-workflows.title: Graph Learning Workflows
graph-learning-workflows.when_to_read: Graph neural network tasks, graph construction, message passing, graph datasets, graph data loaders, graph partitioning, sparse graph operations, and distributed graph training.
graph-learning-workflows.how_to_choose: Use this scenario for graph ML packages; choose DGL for DGL/GraphBolt/DistGraph and PyG for torch-geometric data/model workflows.

image-generation-and-lora-training-workf.title: Image Generation and LoRA Training Workflows
image-generation-and-lora-training-workf.when_to_read: Stable Diffusion or Diffusers pipelines, ComfyUI node graphs, image generation servers, diffusion schedulers/adapters, dataset TOML, LoRA training, and model utilities.
image-generation-and-lora-training-workf.how_to_choose: Choose Diffusers for Python pipeline/model APIs, ComfyUI for graph nodes and server workflows, and sd-scripts for Stable Diffusion LoRA training scripts.

language-model-evaluation-workflows.title: Language Model Evaluation Workflows
language-model-evaluation-workflows.when_to_read: LLM benchmark configuration, evaluation harness runs, OpenCompass configs, task authoring, model backends, result summaries, decontamination, and judge-based evaluation.
language-model-evaluation-workflows.how_to_choose: Choose by benchmark framework: lm-evaluation-harness for EleutherAI harness tasks, OpenCompass for OpenCompass configs/backends, and Evaluate for metric modules or evaluator utilities.

llm-finetuning-training-serving-workflow.title: LLM Fine-Tuning, Training, and Serving Workflows
llm-finetuning-training-serving-workflow.when_to_read: Transformers model usage, local generation, fine-tuning, serving CLIs, PEFT adapters, LlamaFactory/Axolotl/ms-swift training, vLLM/SGLang/LMDeploy serving, BentoML model services, and LLM deployment.
llm-finetuning-training-serving-workflow.how_to_choose: Choose by primary surface: Transformers for core model/tokenizer/trainer APIs, PEFT/bitsandbytes for adapters or quantization, Axolotl/LlamaFactory/ms-swift/Unsloth for training frameworks, vLLM/SGLang/LMDeploy for serving, BentoML for packaging services, and LitGPT for local lightweight workflows.

llm-post-training-rlhf-workflows.title: LLM Post-Training and RLHF Workflows
llm-post-training-rlhf-workflows.when_to_read: SFT, DPO, GRPO, PPO, reward models, RLHF data/rewards, rollout engines, post-training configs, LoRA merging, Ray/Megatron distributed RLHF, and preference optimization backends.
llm-post-training-rlhf-workflows.how_to_choose: Choose this scenario when the request is about preference optimization or RLHF/post-training; choose the generic LLM training scenario for ordinary SFT or serving.

materials-science-and-crystal-structure-.title: Materials Science and Crystal Structure Workflows
materials-science-and-crystal-structure-.when_to_read: Computational materials, crystal structures, entries and phase stability, Materials Project data, surfaces/interfaces, diffraction/spectra, VASP-oriented pymatgen CLIs, and materials-analysis Python workflows.
materials-science-and-crystal-structure-.how_to_choose: Use this scenario when the user names pymatgen or asks for materials-science structure, thermodynamics, external materials data, or crystallographic analysis rather than molecular drug-discovery chemistry or generic array/data workflows.
materials-science-and-crystal-structure-.split_guidance: Existing molecular-ML chemistry scenarios focus on molecules, drug discovery, force fields, retrosynthesis, or trajectory analysis; scientific-python-data-workflows is too generic and does not capture pymatgen's crystal, phase-diagram, Materials Project, and VASP-oriented surfaces. Other computational materials repositories such as atomate, custodian, matminer, ASE-materials workflows, phonopy-adjacent materials tools, or crystal-analysis packages can route to the matching scenario.

medical-imaging-and-segmentation-workflo.title: Medical Imaging and Segmentation Workflows
medical-imaging-and-segmentation-workflo.when_to_read: Medical image transforms, nnU-Net planning/training/inference, MONAI bundles, TorchIO patch workflows, healthcare segmentation, and 3D image augmentation.
medical-imaging-and-segmentation-workflo.how_to_choose: Choose by medical imaging package and workflow: MONAI for healthcare model/bundle workflows, nnU-Net for nnU-Net planning/training/inference, and TorchIO for 3D augmentation and patch sampling.

ml-experiment-tracking-and-mlops-workflo.title: ML Experiment Tracking and MLOps Workflows
ml-experiment-tracking-and-mlops-workflo.when_to_read: Experiment tracking, AutoML/HPO, model registries, artifacts, sweeps, Launch jobs, MLflow projects/models/evaluation, data versioning pipelines, GenAI observability, local serving, and operational MLOps.
ml-experiment-tracking-and-mlops-workflo.how_to_choose: Choose MLflow/W&B for experiment tracking and registries, Optuna/NNI/AutoGluon for optimization or AutoML, DVC for data and pipeline versioning, and BentoML when packaging or serving is the main action.

molecular-ml-drug-discovery-and-chemistr.title: Molecular ML, Drug Discovery, and Chemistry Workflows
molecular-ml-drug-discovery-and-chemistr.when_to_read: Cheminformatics, molecule processing, molecular property prediction, featurization, MoleculeNet, docking, molecular simulation, force fields, conformers, reactions, retrosynthesis, and chemistry model training.
molecular-ml-drug-discovery-and-chemistr.how_to_choose: Choose by chemistry surface: RDKit/Datamol for molecule processing, Chemprop/DeepChem for molecular ML, OpenMM/MDAnalysis for simulation and trajectory analysis, AiZynthFinder for retrosynthesis, and REINVENT for molecular design CLI workflows.

multimodal-training-audio-naflex.title: Multimodal Training, Audio, and NaFlex Workflows
multimodal-training-audio-naflex.when_to_read: OpenCLIP training commands, open_clip_train, CSV/WebDataset data, task-era TrainingTask wrappers, FSDP, torch.compile, CLAP audio, NaFlex, GenLIP, or GenLAP.
multimodal-training-audio-naflex.how_to_choose: Use this scenario for OpenCLIP-specific training or multimodal audio/vision-language workflows that are not just model inference.

protein-structure-prediction-and-design-.title: Protein Structure Prediction and Design Workflows
protein-structure-prediction-and-design-.when_to_read: AlphaFold, ColabFold, Boltz, Chai-1, ESM, ProteinMPNN, RFdiffusion, MSA generation, structure prediction outputs, protein backbone generation, binder design, and sequence design.
protein-structure-prediction-and-design-.how_to_choose: Choose structure predictors for structure output tasks, ESM for protein language model embeddings or variant effects, ProteinMPNN for sequence design, and RFdiffusion for backbone/binder generation.

python-ai-api-services-and-deployment.title: Python AI API Services and Deployment
python-ai-api-services-and-deployment.when_to_read: Python AI packages exposed as APIs, FastAPI services, OpenAI-compatible endpoints, MCP servers, secured services, custom routes, clusters, consoles, Docker/cloud/serverless deployment, or observability targets.
python-ai-api-services-and-deployment.how_to_choose: Use this scenario when serving an AI application API is primary; use model-serving or gateway scenarios when the request is about model backends or provider routing.

python-repository-maintenance.title: Python Repository Maintenance
python-repository-maintenance.when_to_read: Tasks that edit, review, test, document, package, or maintain Python source repositories rather than using them only as libraries.
python-repository-maintenance.how_to_choose: Choose this scenario when the user is contributing to or modifying a repo checkout; otherwise choose the user-facing workflow scenario for the package.

reinforcement-learning-workflows.title: Reinforcement Learning Workflows
reinforcement-learning-workflows.when_to_read: Gymnasium environments, Stable-Baselines3 algorithms, RL vectorization, policies, evaluation, experiment scripts, replay buffers, collectors, action masks, multi-agent RL environments, and PPO-family rollout-agent training.
reinforcement-learning-workflows.how_to_choose: Choose by RL package and layer: Stable-Baselines3 for algorithms, CleanRL for single-file training scripts and operations, Tianshou for collectors/buffers/offline RL, PettingZoo for multi-agent environments, and OpenRLHF/TRL/verl only when the task is LLM post-training.

scientific-python-data-workflows.title: Scientific Python Data Workflows
scientific-python-data-workflows.when_to_read: NumPy-style array analysis combined with molecular simulation trajectories, coordinate transformations, or per-frame scientific computations in Python.
scientific-python-data-workflows.how_to_choose: Use this scenario when the task is scientific data analysis rather than chemistry modeling, drug discovery, or general data pipelines.

single-cell-omics-and-scanpy-workflows.title: Single-Cell Omics and Scanpy Workflows
single-cell-omics-and-scanpy-workflows.when_to_read: AnnData/MuData setup, Scanpy preprocessing/QC, graph analysis, marker genes, probabilistic scvi-tools models, multimodal/spatial omics, and single-cell plotting/reporting.
single-cell-omics-and-scanpy-workflows.how_to_choose: Choose Scanpy for preprocessing, plotting, and AnnData workflows; choose scvi-tools for probabilistic models and deep generative single-cell analysis.

speech-ai-modeling-and-audio-workflows.title: Speech AI Modeling and Audio Workflows
speech-ai-modeling-and-audio-workflows.when_to_read: Speech AI tasks involving ASR, transcription, TTS, vocoders, G2P, speaker diarization, VAD, forced alignment, audio enhancement/restoration, speech language models, voice agents, speech manifests, or speech-model repository maintenance.
speech-ai-modeling-and-audio-workflows.how_to_choose: Choose the repo skill whose speech/audio framework, model family, data format, runtime, or repository workflow most directly matches the request; use package names, checkpoint formats, manifest fields, and task terms such as ASR, TTS, diarization, VAD, forced alignment, SpeechLM, voice-agent, or audio enhancement as strong signals.
speech-ai-modeling-and-audio-workflows.split_guidance: Existing scenarios cover generic LLM training, multimodal/audio contrastive training, dataset processing, and MLOps, but none routes speech-specific ASR, TTS, diarization, audio-to-audio, SpeechLM2, voice-agent, manifest, checkpoint, and NeMo repository-development workflows together. Using an LLM or dataset scenario would hide the speech-domain model, data, and troubleshooting boundaries this skill provides. Future skills for speech recognition, TTS, diarization, audio enhancement, voice-agent, or speech-language-model repositories can share this scenario and compare by framework, model family, data format, and runtime surface.
